meta.purpose: Define scenario triggers and action orientations for TDD judgment.
meta.load_condition: Load when a concrete situation involving test-driven development is detected.

scenes.name: New Feature Implementation
scenes.trigger_signal: User asks to implement a new feature. No tests exist yet.

sub_scenarios.trap_belief: I should plan the full feature first, then write tests for the whole thing.

three_questions.belief: Is full upfront planning more efficient than incremental TDD cycles?
three_questions.state: What is the first observable behavior this feature should exhibit?
three_questions.need: What is the thinnest vertical slice that proves the architecture works end-to-end?

sub_scenarios.action_template: Identify first vertical slice → Write failing test for one behavior → Implement minimally → Refactor → Next slice

avoid: implementing the full feature then writing tests
use: identify the tracer bullet slice first

sub_scenarios.expected_result: Feature built as a series of vertical slices, each with a test that drove its design. No untested behavior.

scenes.name: Bug Fix with Expected Behavior
scenes.trigger_signal: A bug is reported and the expected behavior is clearly defined.

sub_scenarios.trap_belief: I should fix the bug first, then write a test to make sure it doesn't happen again.

three_questions.belief: Will the test accurately capture the bug's conditions if I write it after the fix?
three_questions.state: What is the reproduction condition for this bug?
three_questions.need: Can I write a test that fails with the current code and passes after the fix?

sub_scenarios.action_template: Write a test that reproduces the bug (RED) → Fix the code (GREEN) → Verify test passes → Refactor if needed

avoid: writing a test that only verifies the fix, not the bug
use: the test must fail before the fix and pass after

sub_scenarios.expected_result: Regression test that captures the bug condition. Fix verified by test transition from RED to GREEN.

scenes.name: Horizontal Slicing Detected
scenes.trigger_signal: The agent or user proposes writing all unit tests, then all integration tests, then all E2E tests.

sub_scenarios.trap_belief: Writing all tests at one level before moving to the next is organized and efficient.

three_questions.belief: Does horizontal slicing test real behavior or just layer coverage?
three_questions.state: If a unit test passes but the feature doesn't work end-to-end, what value did the unit test provide?
three_questions.need: Can I reorganize these tests into vertical slices that each verify one complete behavior?

sub_scenarios.action_template: Identify the behaviors being tested → Group into vertical slices → Write one test per slice → Implement one slice at a time

avoid: all unit tests then all integration tests then all E2E tests
use: one vertical slice at a time
each slice tests one behavior from entry to outcome

sub_scenarios.expected_result: Test suite organized by behavior, not by layer. Each test traces one behavior end-to-end.

scenes.name: Over-Mocking Detected
scenes.trigger_signal: A test replaces most or all dependencies with mocks, testing the mock interactions rather than observable behavior.

sub_scenarios.trap_belief: Mocking all dependencies makes the test isolated and therefore reliable.

three_questions.belief: Is this test verifying behavior or verifying that the code calls the mocks in the expected order?
three_questions.state: If the implementation changes but behavior stays the same, will this test break?
three_questions.need: What is the minimal mock boundary that preserves behavioral testing?

sub_scenarios.action_template: Identify which mocks test behavior vs. implementation → Remove implementation-coupled mocks → Test at the behavioral boundary instead

avoid: verifying mock call counts and order
use: verify observable behavior, not interaction patterns

sub_scenarios.expected_result: Tests that verify behavior at boundaries. Implementation can change without breaking tests.
